timeline.label: FY2025 10-K: returned $17.6B to common shareholders; CET1 13.2% vs 11.6% required
FY2025 record revenue $85.2B across five operating businesses; RoTCE 7.7%
Transformation and technology spend ~$3.3B (+14% YoY) as Jane Fraser simplification program continues
Jane Fraser became CEO; ongoing exit of non-core consumer franchises and organizational simplification
Stress Capital Buffer (SCB) framework links capital return to Fed stress-test outcomes
Post-crisis restructuring and TARP-era dilution; multi-decade simplification from universal bank model

industry.structure: Global systemically important banks compete on deposit franchises, trading and banking scale, and regulatory capital efficiency; top-tier US peers include JPMorgan, Bank of America, and Wells Fargo.
industry.share_shift: Citigroup is shrinking non-core consumer footprints while investing in Services, Markets, and US Personal Banking; peers with stronger domestic deposit franchises trade at higher price-to-tangible-book multiples.
industry.trend: Higher rates support net interest income but credit normalization, regulatory capital rules, and technology transformation costs pressure returns on tangible common equity.
